quote_text: "Verily at the first Chaos came to be, but then broad-bosomed Gaia, ever-sure foundation of all the deathless ones... and dim Tartarus in the depth of the wide-pathed Earth, and Eros... And Gaia first

"But Ouranos... used to hide them [his children] all away in a secret place of Gaia... And vast Gaia groaned within, being straitened... She created a new metal, grey adamant, and made a great sickle,

"Great Kronos swallowed each child as it issued from the womb... And Rhea was greatly distressed. She devised a plan: she hid Zeus in a cave on Crete and gave Kronos a great stone wrapped in swaddling
"So they [Titans and gods] fought with one another for ten full years... Then Zeus no longer held back his might, but he attacked with all his strength, and from Olympos he came, hurling thunderbolts

"From Typhoeus come all the storm-blasts... but when Zeus had conquered him, he lashed him with a whip, and crushed him, and cast him into wide Tartarus." (Theogony 869-880)

"Prometheus... stole from Zeus the fire of the Sun... He deceived Zeus, offering him bones wrapped in fat while keeping the good meat for men... Zeus, angered, hid fire; Prometheus stole it in a fenne

"Hephaestus at Zeus's command made from earth the likeness of a modest maiden. Athena dressed her, the Charites and Peitho adorned her, Hermes gave her a cunning mind and treacherous heart, and a lyin

"The immortals on Olympos made a golden race of mortal men... they lived like gods without sorrow of heart... After them a second race, of silver, worse... they could not keep from sin... Then a third

"And then Zeus brought great misery upon the men of the Bronze Age... He caused a great flood... Deucalion built a chest/ark, stored provisions, and sailed for nine days and nights until landing on Pa
